speaker: MATT
raw_text: Anyway. Before we get into that, let's get to our announcements for the episode. First and foremost, guys, we have until April 18th, we have less than a week left before the Critical Role survey is closed. So if you hadn't had a chance yet to help us out, let us know where you're from, what you like about the show, what kind of things you'd like to see in the future about the show. You can go check it out--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We're going to go ahead and hop into this episode soon. Ashley Johnson is on her way. She'll be joining us shortly, but she's in traffic. Coming from Santa Monica.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Ashley Johnson!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But she's returned, I believe. Like, returned-returned?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: For a bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: For a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For a while? So that's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, we can't be sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She could die tonight.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why would you say that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm just saying!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What are you--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's true.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm just saying.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Take it back!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike could possibly--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That would be really bad, by the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That'd be really bad if suddenly Pike, like, 'welcome back,' and then Pike just explodes in the game. It'd be pretty bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Spontaneous combustion.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Which, by the way, you should get Scott McCloud on there. If you have a connection, let me know. Not on Wednesday, but at some point in the future, I should-- Anyway, we'll talk later. All right. So also Satine Phoenix's first run on GM Tips went up today on YouTube. If you have a chance, check it out. Happily have passed the torch onto her. She's a dear friend for many years, an awesome girl with a lot of awesome nerd cred. And she's bringing her own flavor spin and her majestic presence to the new run of DM Tips. So go ahead, check it out. She has a lot of awesome things to say. And thank you again, Satine, for doing that and being awesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, Wednesday Club. We're finally-- Because I forgot that I was leaving town for some reason that I don't remember at all. I don't remember why I had to leave town for some reason.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Die. Just die.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Yeah, we did our funny book show last week, so this week we're going to do our Comics 101. It's going to be a lot of fun. We're going to talk about the basics of how to read comics, if you're brand new to it or if you have questions. It'll be fun. We're going to talk about the amount of time that moves between panels, thought bubbles, talk bubbles, all the fun stuff. It'll be great.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You should bring a copy of Understanding Comics.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you should.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes, Understanding Comics is going to be talked about.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Perfect.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's a really, really great book.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So, as you all arrive, touch down, the familiar scent. The smiles come over you, knowing that you've managed to successfully return to a comfortable homestead. The sky is broken with clouds. But you can still see some of the dark blue behind it starting to turn purples and oranges, as the sun, though out of sight, is hitting dusk. A few people look over and glance and you hear whispers as people begin to recognize your arrival with a flashy spark of light and that slight Terminator-like slow rise from a crouch position as you all pull out of the other side of the portal.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not your portrait.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know who that is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, Scanlan. Sorry. We're still getting over it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You know, that guy that played at your father's whatever.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It's been a while, and now that things have calmed down, and from what we've heard that you've all saved the city, I guess, we're going to bring the children back to their home. We're going to be taking the Parchwood Way to the Silvercut Roadway, and we have guardians, we have people we've hired or have agreed to come along and keep us safe. The children have taken a shine to the boys, as if you haven't noticed. If it's all right, we're thinking of bringing them with us."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Uh-huh!" They both continue off after the kids just as Salda Tal'Dorei walks up from between some of the Pale Guard that are currently helping load things onto these carts. And she sees you all, and comes forth, "Oh, my goodness! It's so great to see you all safe and sound!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Your majesty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hello.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The last few times you've seen her, she's looked a little frazzled but still with what remains of her formal attire which she had on her after the attack on Tal'Dorei. Right now, she's dressed very simply. You wouldn't be able to tell her from most of the regular casual farmfolk, and you would have no instinct of the noble lifestyle that she comes from, and it suits her well. She smiles and greets, and gives a respectful bow. "So we're heading back to Emon."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They'll be happy to have you.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Yeah. We've done as best as we could to shield them from the brunt of the past few months, but things are cheerier here, definitely, and I think they've taken that upon themselves as well."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's wonderful.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's very kind of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Quite gracious.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We have the Alabaster Lyceum-- once it's up and running again, of course-- and I know the headmaster there. We can provide a good education for the boys, which, upon talking with them, I think they're both in dire need of."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you need any help with that? We feel a bit of responsibility for them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Really, it's fine. You guys have done enough anyway, and to be honest, I believe your sister needs your help here more than we could on the journey there."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do your kids-- holding up okay?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Anyway, I think we're preparing to set out first thing in the morning, and I just want to say thank you for everything."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do your kids-- holding up okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah. We've done as best as we could to shield them from the brunt of the past few months, but things are cheerier here, definitely, and I think they've taken that upon themselves as well."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just be prepared, the Emon you left isn't necessarily the Emon you'll be returning to. It's getting better, but it takes time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can assure you that my memory of the place, at least the moments before we left, couldn't be any more dire than we could return to. Any semblance of calm at our home is welcome. But--" and she glances over-- "I think Odessa has a crush on Hunin. I don't want to break her heart by keeping them too far away, so that's--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Cool mom.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So Pike, having come along, as you've been helping out with the reconstruction effort in Emon and was notified that some of the remaining royals of the family of the Sovereign were going to make their way towards Emon, the least you could do was be a part of that. So you ushered a number of the travel carts that were intended for this part of the caravan, since Whitestone had to maintain most of their hardware within the city to remain functional, and you just arrived in the city a few days ago, and as you've been helping these people prepare and organize the whole endeavor, you just watch Vox Machina, your family, walk by and start making their way towards one of the taverns just as the sun's setting. And you rush up to catch up with them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I get on my broom and continue the journey.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Had a little too much of Percy's hooch? What's the matter?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys start making your way towards the tavern, you hear a series of heavy metallic footfalls behind you with accelerated pace, quickening, quickening. And you glance over your shoulder and rushing from the caravan, apparently in the process of helping it being set up and part of the party that brought or is spearheading the organization of this caravan, you see a small gnomish cleric in plate armor. (cheering)

preceding_turns.text: Hello!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Come on in!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: She's back, baby! She's back!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Ah! Hello, everybody!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is in character, by the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How was traffic?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, hi! Oh, just so terrible!

preceding_turns.text: With the caravan!

preceding_turns.text: Hello, everybody!

preceding_turns.text: Hi!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you guys make your way into the Tipsy Quorum, the warm interior is comfortable to you guys. You recall most of it, as most of the time you've spent in there has been in various states of intoxication. There's a beautiful hearth on the north end of the complex. I say "complex" because it's a series of rooms. There's the main tavern area in which the drinking happens. There's a small eating area that's separate from that, so they keep the localized tavern central area on its own. There's a kitchen, an open kitchen, so all the smoke and the food smell comes just barreling through. It's pretty sparse, mainly because a lot of the people are in the process of helping with the tavern, or they just haven't quite hit that point in the evening. Dusk is when people start finishing their work for the day, and they usually come in in the next couple hours or so. But you do see a couple of the Pale Guard are currently just taking a break. There's a handful of farmhands that are just in there, finishing a small breakfast and having some drinks. There's the bartender and a few bar handlers, a barmaid just waiting for customers to come in. And as you enter, immediately she goes from leaning on the back wall. "Oh, hah, sorry!" And she comes up and shows you guys to-- "Any table you like!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, or, "I want to mate with you." That's probably bad as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just don't use the word "mate," not even in the buddy sense.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, "you're my mate."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: "You're my mate."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's okay, that's all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm feeling confident.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Remember, I'm a Darrington. I can walk into any establishment and find a young lass who has heard of me and take her home for the night.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, well.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: There it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Good, good, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Well, there it is. That's what we're looking for.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right, cool, that's good, yeah, that's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I felt pretty good on that one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hard cut! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can we do that? Is that something you can do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Smash cut to--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's Dungeons & Dragons, man!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Rejection, rejection, rejection!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The best montage ever.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Roll for montage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Roll for montage!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About 15 minutes go by and you guys are having a conversation, you're catching Pike up with what you guys have been doing in the city of Dis and all the chaos, and in the middle of telling an incredible story about how you ran into Tova, right as you get to that point, all of a sudden plates (clanging) at the table and you can see these beautiful turkey legs, fresh out of the oven. They're still sizzling on the outside. She says, "Careful now, they're hot. Do you need another drink? Anyone else?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Why, whatever for?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: My darling, it's because I'm so ripped.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She can't stop the smile, a grin curls on her face, she goes, (laughs).

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like us at the table should actually get to rate the come ons. The females should get to rate these.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'll see what I can do."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I think it's a great idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Beware, this is a no-win zone. Don't get too close.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I want in on this as well now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, you're jumping in on this?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm not getting on this, I just want in on the rating.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah, on the rating.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She heads off. She turns around and heads back to the kitchen.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: 50 gold says she hates all of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I technically can't bet because I do actually know everyone enough to--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, here, right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I have a little more inside knowledge than one should.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is she going to hate all of them?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: God, I hope so. (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I have about 30 minutes or so before the rest of this place fills up with folks far less interesting than you, so unfortunately, I've got to be ready for the rush. Your words are sweet and flowery, but I think I may be a little old for you, sonny." Slides the drink back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This is legit the twins over here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's definitely a long pause. "Wow." (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And her hand reaches over and grabs your mug and slides it away. "So I think we're cutting him off." (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Man, why, I think that's a good idea. Would you like to take a load off? Join us for a bit, I'm sure your legs are super tired.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "My hobbies involve raising two children after Darren left about ten years back, and working hard to make some money and hopefully one day get out of this shithole tavern and maybe start a business doing some sort of, I don't know, textile work. Which, by the way, I brought your thread and needle." And she throws down what looks like a half-done, shoddy knitting job.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At which point, she goes back to the table to bring you your third round. As she does, she goes and returns all the mugs to the table, finishing with yours. She slides it forward to you and gives you a wink.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: A wink?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Later tonight, player!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry we got off on the wrong foot earlier.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) "It's okay."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I was taken by your obvious beauty, but there's so much more to you than that, I'm sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (whispers) Come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: If I could--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: One thing about herself!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Tell me about yourself. How do you feel about-- What do you do during the day when the pub's not open?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She's taken aback for a moment. Looks at everyone else at the table with a "who are you people?" look.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Matt. What does she do during the day when the pub's not open? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm a writer and a reader. What are your hobbies?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you guys are having this conversation, Taryon, you feel a hand hit your shoulder and turn you back a little bit. It's Shauna. She leans forward and says, "Listen, honey. If you're looking to go ahead and find yourself an evening, wait here about 30 minutes. I'll introduce you to someone." And she releases your shoulder and goes back to work.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh god. Doty was a machine made of metal.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're not judging.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, it's not judging.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It would have been my first question, too. I'm not going to lie.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Really?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It was a terrible joke.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We've never met anybody like Doty before.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was a bit awkward with girls before I started out.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Really?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You? Debonair?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I had a lot of sisters.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Royal?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well, we end up sheltered, don't we?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's got really good technique. He always brings a bottle of liquor with him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I learn very quickly. She's an excellent teacher. Never mind. But no, adventuring and going out into the world, you learn a lot. You meet people. You get out. It's good for you. Look at all these people.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I was very sheltered-- (gasps) were all three of us sheltered on this side?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We are sheltered children on this side.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yay, sheltered kids! Hey. Awkward.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At that point, she smiles very widely and you can see the mother poke through for a minute from the barmaid. She nods and says, "Look, it's all right. Trust me, I deal with far worse from most of these riffraff." She points to most of the other tables. "It's fine."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is Shauna around at all?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, she's in the process of running around.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I run up and snag her for a second?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure. She sets down a couple of drinks. "I feel like we did our good deed for the night."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like it happened. It definitely happened. Question, though. How long does your friend usually stay with her cohorts? Does it-- Is it--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't time it, if that's what your question is. I don't know!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does she spend the night with them, or is it like an in and out-- Do you see her again, around, quickly?
